description: Create test/Router.Fees.t.sol exercising FR-3/4/5/6/7: protocol fee (cap + arithmetic + custody in Router), partner fee on input (transferred before executor call; cap enforced independently), partner fee on output (transferred after slippage cap; reverts if it pushes amountOut below outputMin), positive slippage capture (capped at outputQuote; router retains surplus) vs passPositiveSlippageToUser pass-through (user receives full amount), and combined fee + positive-slippage scenarios.
commit: test(router): add protocol/partner fee and positive-slippage tests
steps: Create test/Router.Fees.t.sol extending the same setUp as Router.t.sol (duplicate setUp or factor into test/helpers/RouterTestBase.sol). Add helpers _mkParams(...) that build SwapParams with defaults and allow per-field overrides.

Protocol fee tests: test_ProtocolFee_Charged (50 bps applied to 1000e18 input -> 5e18 retained in router, 995e18 forwarded); test_ProtocolFee_ZeroBps (no fee, full amount forwarded); test_ProtocolFee_CapExceeds_Reverts (201 bps reverts ProtocolFeeExceedsCap(201)); test_ProtocolFee_AtCap (200 bps succeeds, 2% retained); test_ProtocolFee_AccumulatesInRouter (two consecutive swaps sum fees correctly in router balance); test_NoFees_EmitsZeroFeeFields (protocolFeeBps=0 and partnerFeeBps=0 with produced==outputQuote: via vm.recordLogs decode the Swap event and assert protocolFee == 0, partnerFee == 0, positiveSlippageCaptured == 0, partnerRecipient == address(0) -- locks in edge-case row 3 of the spec).

Partner fee on input tests: test_PartnerFeeInput_Paid (caller supplies partnerFeeBps=100, partnerRecipient=alice; alice receives 10e18 for 1000e18 input pre-executor; executor gets 990e18 - protocolFee); test_PartnerFeeInput_CapExceeds_Reverts; test_PartnerFeeInput_ZeroRecipient_Reverts (InvalidPartnerRecipient when bps > 0 && recipient == address(0)); test_PartnerFeeInput_StacksWithProtocolFee (both deducted from input, arithmetic verified).

Partner fee on output tests: test_PartnerFeeOutput_Paid (produced=1000e18, partnerFeeBps=100 -> partnerRecipient gets 10e18, user gets 990e18, deducted after slippage cap); test_PartnerFeeOutput_AfterSlippageCap (amountOut=1100, outputQuote=1000 -> capped to 1000, partner fee 10, user gets 990, router retains 100); test_PartnerFeeOutput_PushesBelowOutputMin_Reverts (SlippageExceeded).

Positive slippage tests: test_PositiveSlippage_CapturedWhenFlagOff (MockDEX returns 1100, outputQuote=1000, user receives 1000, router retains 100); test_PositiveSlippage_PassThroughWhenFlagOn (user receives 1100); test_PositiveSlippage_NoSurplus_Noop (amountOut == outputQuote); test_PositiveSlippage_AtOutputQuoteExactly_NeitherRetainedNorForfeited.

Fuzz test: testFuzz_NoSilentShortPayment(uint256 produced, uint256 quote, uint256 minOut, uint16 protoBps, uint16 partnerBps, bool onOutput) bounding inputs to [1, 1e30] and bps to [0, 200]; asserts the final user amount is >= minOut whenever tx succeeds and that tx reverts with SlippageExceeded otherwise.
Run `forge test --match-path test/Router.Fees.t.sol` with `-vvv` and confirm all pass.
acceptance_criteria: [auto] File test/Router.Fees.t.sol exists; `forge test --match-path test/Router.Fees.t.sol` passes.
[auto] grep -n 'testFuzz_NoSilentShortPayment' test/Router.Fees.t.sol matches exactly one line.

[auto] grep -n 'Router.ProtocolFeeExceedsCap.selector\|Router.PartnerFeeExceedsCap.selector\|Router.InvalidPartnerRecipient.selector\|Router.SlippageExceeded.selector' test/Router.Fees.t.sol returns at least 4 matches.
[auto] Fuzz test uses `bound(_, 0, 200)` for bps parameters (verifiable by grep -n 'bound(.*200)' test/Router.Fees.t.sol).
[auto] All tests pass under `forge test --match-path test/Router.Fees.t.sol -v`; fuzz runs default 256 iterations per foundry.toml with no failures.
[manual] Each positive-slippage test asserts BOTH the user's final balance AND the router's retained balance to lock in capture semantics.
implementation_notes: Added test/Router.Fees.t.sol (18 tests, 17 `test_` + 1 fuzz, 257 fuzz runs) covering FR-3/4/5/6/7: protocol fee arithmetic + cap + Router custody + zero-bps edge case + accumulation + zero-fee event shape; partner-fee-on-input with cap, zero-recipient revert, stacking with protocol fee; partner-fee-on-output post-slippage-cap ordering with SlippageExceeded revert when fee pushes below outputMin; positive-slippage capture/pass-through/no-surplus/exact-quote (each asserts both user and router balances per the manual criterion); and a no-silent-short-payment fuzz with `bound(_, 0, 200)` for bps. Commit 3a9e90f.
